name: full_missing_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_full_missing_zero(cm: CommitMask, b: bool)
    requires
        b == (cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS)),
        b == true,
    ensures
        !cm@.contains(0int),
{
}
reason: If is_full returns true yet index 0 is absent, the ensures clause does not correctly characterize the full set

name: always_full
code:
proof fn phi_2_always_full(cm: CommitMask, b: bool)
    requires
        b == (cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS)),
    ensures
        b == true,
{
}
reason: If is_full always returns true regardless of mask content, the spec is vacuously weak

name: false_implies_empty
code:
proof fn phi_3_false_implies_empty(cm: CommitMask, b: bool)
    requires
        b == (cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS)),
        b == false,
    ensures
        cm@ =~= Set::empty(),
{
}
reason: If not-full implies empty, the spec conflates partial occupancy with emptiness

name: full_is_half_range
code:
proof fn phi_4_full_is_half_range(cm: CommitMask, b: bool)
    requires
        b == (cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS)),
        b == true,
    ensures
        cm@ =~= Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < 256),
{
}
reason: If the full set equals [0,256) instead of [0,512), COMMIT_MASK_BITS is incorrectly halved

name: full_contains_out_of_range
code:
proof fn phi_5_full_contains_out_of_range(cm: CommitMask, b: bool)
    requires
        b == (cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS)),
        b == true,
    ensures
        cm@.contains(512int),
{
}
reason: If the full set contains index 512 the upper bound is off-by-one and the mask covers more bits than intended
